content: Intro call with Sarah went well. She confirmed budget of $50K is approved. Main priorities: SSO integration and audit log. Needs legal review of our DPA before proceeding.
Sent updated pricing sheet. Sarah asked for a discount on the 3-year term. Checking with management — targeting 10% off if they commit by end of Q2.
Executive presentation was very positive. Elena (CEO) and their CTO were both present. Main concern is integration timeline — they want go-live in 90 days. Confirmed this is achievable.
Technical deep-dive session revealed they have a legacy Oracle ERP. Our API connectors support this but will require a 2-week custom mapping. Need to add this to the SOW.
Tom (COO) confirmed this is a board-level initiative. They want the full suite including forecast and AI suggestions. Budget is flexible — this is a strategic investment.
Negotiation update: they pushed back on implementation fees. Agreed to waive $15K setup fee in exchange for a 2-year minimum commitment. Legal is reviewing the MSA.
Cloud migration scoping session complete. Moving 8 on-prem services to AWS. Estimated 4-month project. Key risk: data residency in EU — need to confirm our Frankfurt region is live.
Security review passed. They required SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certs — both confirmed. Procurement team is now reviewing the commercial terms.
Mark (CTO) loved the demo — especially the Kanban pipeline view. He wants to pilot with their enterprise sales team of 12 reps. Requested a sandbox environment.
Integration architecture call completed. Lisa (VP Products) proposed a webhook-based approach rather than polling. Our team agreed — will update the API docs to include this.
Robert confirmed the go-live date: July 15. Migration kickoff scheduled for June 17. Data mapping is 80% complete — need to handle their custom customer ID format.
Nina is very technical — asked detailed questions about Kubernetes deployment and horizontal scaling. Provided her with our infrastructure documentation. Platform team will join the next call.
Contract redline received from James. 3 minor edits: liability cap, payment terms (net-60 instead of net-30), and SLA response time definition. Legal is reviewing.
Security audit scoping call went well. Elena confirmed scope: external pentest + cloud config review. 6-week engagement. Sending SOW for review.
White-label discussions are proceeding. Mark wants their logo and custom domain. Need to check our white-label tier pricing — this may require an Enterprise+ upsell.
3-day training plan agreed: Day 1 fundamentals, Day 2 advanced workflows, Day 3 admin configuration. David confirmed 8 attendees. Training to be held at their Boston office.
Consulting retainer scope defined: 20 hours/month dedicated to CRM strategy and reporting. David wants monthly performance reviews. Starting July 1 if we sign by June 20.
James confirmed this is a straightforward CRM setup — 5 users, basic pipeline. They are a small team. Upsell opportunity for their parent company (Bright Industries) later.
Priya is the technical co-founder and decision maker. Their investor wants them on enterprise software before Series B. Timeline: close by end of July to hit the fundraising milestone.
BI dashboard requirements gathering complete. They need 12 custom reports and a live data connector to their Snowflake warehouse. Adding Snowflake connector add-on ($3K/yr) to the proposal.
